supported_hypotheses: Permissive hedging metric is structurally inflated
failed_assumptions: 98.6% hedging rate reflects genuine parent-latent recovery
candidates.notes: MAJOR FINDING: Strict hedging rate (6.2%) is dramatically lower than permissive (98.6%). 93.8% of FN tokens have NONE of the 5 parent-associated latents firing at L0=176. However, these tokens are NOT false negatives at L0=176 (still_fn_176=False), meaning FN resolution at higher L0 happens through mechanisms OTHER than parent-specific latent activation. True strict rate (6.2%) is statistically higher than shuffled control (3.4%, p=0.0004), indicating a small but real signal of parent-specific hedging. Letter G is a notable anomaly (90.5% strict hedging). The 98.6% headline claim needs major qualification.
